steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: $x^{2}-\frac{25}{81}=0$ asks how many distinct real values of $x$ satisfy the equation. Isolate $x^{2}$: $$x^{2}=\frac{25}{81}.$$ The right side is positive, so take square roots.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Zero': $x^{2}=\frac{25}{81}>0$ has real solutions, so zero solutions is impossible. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Exactly one': A positive right side produces two square-root values ($+$ and $-$), not one. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Exactly two': $x=+\frac{5}{9}$ and $x=-\frac{5}{9}$ are both real and distinct. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Infinitely many': A square equation has at most two solutions. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
